checks.a_analysis: The output JSON accurately reflects the section headings and the textual content of the tables that were successfully converted. The alt text for images is AI-generated as allowed. No evidence of completely fabricated paragraphs or invented sections was found.
I reviewed the JSON for URLs, phone numbers, and addresses. The URL present in the Symantec training section matches the source exactly. No other contact information was extracted or fabricated.

All 17 pages of the source PDF are represented in the JSON metadata and via section headings or image placeholders. While content within sections is severely truncated or omitted from the structured text (see C4 and C5), no entire section or page is completely absent.

Multiple tables in the output are missing the vast majority of their rows compared to the source. Specifically: 1) The 'Carahsoft Trellix' table for lines 10-29 contains only line 10 (1 out of 20 rows). 2) The 'Carahsoft Trellix' table for lines 30-43 contains only line 30 (1 out of 14 rows). 3) The 'SHI CowdStrike' table contains only one row ('Cloud Subscription') instead of the 8 data rows in the source. 4) The 'PRESIDIO Cisco' table contains only the header and zero data rows, whereas the source contains 10 rows of data. These are significant content failures.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 12 (Page 14 of 19) contains lines 10 through 29 (20 rows). Page 13 (Page 15 of 19) contains lines 30 through 43 (14 rows). Page 16 (Page 18 of 19) contains a table with 8 rows of cost items. Page 17 (Page 19 of 19) contains a table with 10 rows of data.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 3, Table index 4 (Lines 10-29) has 1 data row. Section 3, Table index 6 (Lines 30-43) has 1 data row. Section 5, Table index 0 has 1 data row. Section 6, Table index 0 has 0 data rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to extract structured text and table data for several entire sections, providing only image placeholders. Most notably, the 'SOPHOS' section (Section 2), which spans 5 pages of complex pricing data in the source, contains only images and no structured tables. Similarly, the first page of the 'Carahsoft Trellix' list (Lines 1-9) is provided only as an image.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source Pages 5-9 (Sophos Price List) consist of 5 pages of detailed pricing tables. Source Page 13 (Lines 1-9 of Carahsoft Trellix list) is a data-rich table.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 2 contains only 5 image elements. Section 3 contains an image (index 3) representing the first page of the Carahsoft list.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor OCR differences and formatting changes are present but do not alter the meaning of the extracted information. Typographical errors in the source (e.g., 'CowdStrike' instead of 'CrowdStrike' on page 18) were faithfully reproduced.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion result was reviewed for duplicated text blocks. While headers and some titles appear multiple times in the source (e.g., 'ENDPOINT RESELLER PRICE LIST' on pages 1, 2, 7, and 9), this reflects the original document's structure and is an intentional refrain. No evidence was found of text being duplicated in the output that appears only once in the source document.

The heading hierarchy generally follows a logical progression from section titles to subheadings. While there is minor inconsistency across sections regarding the presence of explicit Level 2 headings versus immediate Level 3 headings, the progression remains navigable. There are no instances where heading levels are skipped (e.g., moving directly from H1 to H3) in a way that violates accessibility standards. Heading level adjustments for screen reader normalization are permitted per instructions.

The table structural organization is significantly corrupted in several areas. First, in the 'BROADCOM' section, the 'Other costs' information block and the 'PROFESSIONAL SERVICES' table (Source Page 2) are merged into a single table object in the output (index 3 of Section 1). This incorrectly combines distinct sections and misaligns table headers. Second, in the 'TRELLIX' section, the 'Carahsoft Trellix' tables (Source Pages 14 and 15) are structurally truncated; for instance, the table for 'Lines 10-29' contains only one row (Line 10) instead of the sequence of 20 rows in the source. Finally, numerous tables (Pages 5-11, 13) are rendered as 'image' elements, failing to provide the semantic table structure required for accessibility and data extraction.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source Page 2: 'Other costs, if any...' and 'PROFESSIONAL SERVICES' are distinct sections with their own headers. Source Page 14: Table contains 20 rows (Lines 10-29).
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 1, element index 3: A single table merges 'Other costs' and 'Professional Services'. Section 3, table under heading-9: Only one row (Line 10) is present.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the output follows the page-by-page flow of the source PDF. Information is presented in the sequence intended by the original document (Broadcom -> Sophos -> Trellix -> CrowdStrike -> Presidio). Minor reordering or omissions do not materially disrupt the logical flow of the extracted document sections.

An unordered list exists in the 'Professional Services' section on page 11 of the source. The conversion result correctly captures these four items as a list structure, preserving the formatting and meaning.

Multiple URLs visible in the source document are missing from the semantic link structure of the JSON. Specifically, URLs found on pages 7 and 9 of the PDF (within the Sophos section) were not extracted as clickable link objects because those pages were rendered only as images. While the main URL on page 1 was correctly captured, the omission of these support and documentation links is a defect.
checks.b_evidence_output: The URL is visible in image-4 but is not present as a link object in the JSON text structure.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML fails to maintain the layout and information density of the source PDF. Large, multi-page tables spanning several sections (Sophos, Trellix, CrowdStrike, Cisco) have been either entirely omitted, replaced with broken 1x1 pixel images, or severely truncated to a single row. This leads to a massive loss of visual flow and information architecture compared to the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains comprehensive price lists for Sophos (pages 3-7), Trellix (pages 8-13), CrowdStrike (pages 14-16), and Cisco (page 17) with detailed multi-row tables.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML sections corresponding to these pages (e.g., DLT CrowdStrike, Presidio Cisco, and the 'Continued' Trellix sections) show either empty tables, tables with only one row of data, or broken 1x1 pixel image placeholders.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main content area, from Section 2 onwards.

checks.a_analysis: Multiple tables are critically misrendered or truncated. The 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued)' sections for lines 10-29 and 30-43 only render the first row of each set, missing dozens of line items. The 'DLT CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form Table' only contains one row, whereas the source has nine. Most notably, the 'PRESIDIO Cisco Cost Proposal Form' table is rendered as a header with no data rows at all.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source PDF Page 12 (20 rows), Page 13 (14 rows), Page 14 (9 rows), and Page 17 (data rows for Cisco).
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML tables for 'Carahsoft Trellix... Lines 10-29' (1 row), 'Lines 30-43' (1 row), 'DLT CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form Table' (1 row), and 'PRESIDIO Cisco Cost Proposal Form' (0 data rows).

checks.a_analysis: In the Sophos section (Section 2), the conversion relies on images to represent complex tables. However, three consecutive images under the 'SHI (Sophos) Price List (Continued)' heading are broken, pointing to 1x1 pixel transparent base64 data. This results in the complete loss of data from pages 4, 5, and 6 of the original PDF.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source PDF pages 4, 5, and 6 contain essential pricing tables for Sophos products.

checks.a_analysis: No instances of genuine body content duplication (e.g., identical paragraphs or tables repeated within the body) were observed. Structural repetitions such as headings in the TOC and body are acceptable.
